description: EditorPanel renders TWO static, always-present validation hints with `aria-hidden='true'` and `display: none` purely to satisfy `editor-validation.dom.vitest.ts` which does `target.querySelector('[data-testid="block-validation-hint"]')`. The hints are rendered unconditionally, in any state (idle/editing/saving/switching/save-failed), regardless of whether a block validation error has actually occurred. REQ-EDIT-038 / PROP-EDIT-043 require the hint to be shown 'near the affected Block' when `BlockOperationError.kind === 'incompatible-content-for-type'` or `BlockContentError.kind === 'control-character'` — i.e., conditionally on the domain reporting an error. The implementation surfaces no such error data; the snapshot DTO does not carry a block-error field; the hints are decorative DOM purely for the grep test. This is a tautological test pattern: the test asserts the existence of an element that always exists. The actual REQ-EDIT-038 conditional-display behaviour is unverified.

evidence.snippet: このブロック種別に変換できません

suggestedRemediation: Remove the always-present hint elements. Define a real error surface on EditingSessionStateDto (or as a separate inbound channel) that carries BlockOperationError/BlockContentError instances per affected blockId. Render the hint conditionally only when a matching error is present, anchored near the block via the block-wrapper. Update editor-validation.dom.vitest.ts to drive an error snapshot through `_emitState` and assert the hint appears only then.
